# API, Data, Security, and Performance Checklist: Generate 1-Day Demand Forecast

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of UC-03 requirements for API, data, security, and performance concerns before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-03-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](/root/311-forecast-system/specs/003-daily-demand-forecast/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## API Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK001 Are all forecast API surfaces and their distinct responsibilities clearly specified for trigger, run-status, and current-forecast access? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, FR-014, FR-015; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK002 Are authentication and authorization requirements defined per API surface, including any difference between forecast generation and read access? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, FR-015; Plan §Implementation Step 8; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK003 Are response expectations defined for all relevant API failure classes, including unauthorized, forbidden, not found, invalid request, missing input data, engine failure, and storage failure? [Gap, Spec §FR-011-FR-015; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK004 Is the reuse-versus-regenerate decision specified clearly enough that an API consumer can tell when a request should return the current forecast instead of producing a new one? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002-FR-004; Spec §User Story 2; Contract §ForecastRunStatus]

## Data Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK005 Are the forecast data entities and lineage relationships defined clearly enough to distinguish the active approved dataset marker from the active forecast marker? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities; Data Model §Reused Entities; Data Model §CurrentForecastMarker]
- [x] CHK006 Are identity and uniqueness rules specified for forecast runs, forecast versions, hourly buckets, and the current forecast marker? [Completeness, Data Model §ForecastRun; Data Model §ForecastVersion; Data Model §ForecastBucket; Data Model §CurrentForecastMarker]
- [x] CHK007 Are the hourly bucket requirements specific enough to remove ambiguity about interval boundaries, 24-bucket coverage, service-category segmentation, and optional geography omission? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications; Spec §FR-004-FR-007; Data Model §ForecastBucket]
- [x] CHK008 Are requirements for historical forecast retention, failed-run records, and version history explicitly documented or intentionally excluded? [Gap, Spec §Assumptions; Spec §FR-008-FR-014; Data Model §ForecastRun; Data Model §ForecastVersion]

## Security Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK009 Are all protected forecast resources identified consistently across the specification, implementation plan, and contract surfaces? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, FR-015; Plan §Constitution Check Security; Plan §Implementation Step 8; Contract §Paths]
- [x] CHK010 Are data protection requirements specified clearly enough to prohibit raw source payloads, feature matrices, and secrets from appearing in API responses, logs, or operational summaries? [Completeness, Plan §Constraints; Plan §Implementation Step 9; Contract §summary]
- [x] CHK011 Is the boundary between authentication/authorization requirements and general operational failure handling explicit enough to avoid ambiguity in the term "failed" across security and non-security cases? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011-FR-015; Data Model §ForecastRun; Contract §ForecastRunStatus]
- [x] CHK012 Are unauthorized, forbidden, and missing-resource expectations consistent between the written requirements and the forecast API contract? [Consistency, Contract §Paths; Spec §FR-015; Gap]

## Performance Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK013 Are performance requirements quantified for both on-demand forecast generation and current-forecast availability rather than described only in broad terms? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001; Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK014 Are performance expectations defined for alternate flows such as current-forecast reuse and successful category-only forecasts? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003, FR-007; Spec §SC-003-SC-005]
- [x] CHK015 Are the requirements explicit about which performance targets apply only to successful runs and which, if any, apply to failure or degraded paths? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001-SC-005; Plan §Performance Goals]
- [x] CHK016 Are concurrency, scheduling overlap, or load assumptions for scheduled and on-demand forecast requests documented or intentionally excluded? [Gap, Spec §FR-001-FR-004; Plan §Scale/Scope]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
